Handles one command line during command list definition.
tbreak [ ([filename:]lineno | function) [, condition] ]         Same arguments a
Check whether specified line seems to be executable.          Return `lineno` if
enable bpnumber [bpnumber ...]         Enables the breakpoints given as a space
disable bpnumber [bpnumber ...]         Disables the breakpoints given as a spac
condition bpnumber [condition]         Set a new condition for the breakpoint, a
ignore bpnumber [count]         Set the ignore count for the given breakpoint nu
cl(ear) filename:lineno\ncl(ear) [bpnumber [bpnumber...]]         With a space s
w(here)         Print a stack trace, with the most recent frame at the bottom.
u(p) [count]         Move the current frame count (default one) levels up in the
d(own) [count]         Move the current frame count (default one) levels down in
unt(il) [lineno]         Without argument, continue execution until the line wit
s(tep)         Execute the current line, stop at the first possible occasion
n(ext)         Continue execution until the next line in the current function
run [args...]         Restart the debugged python program. If a string is suppli
r(eturn)         Continue execution until the current function returns.
c(ont(inue))         Continue execution, only stop when a breakpoint is encounte
j(ump) lineno         Set the next line that will be executed.  Only available i
debug code         Enter a recursive debugger that steps through the code
q(uit)\nexit         Quit from the debugger. The program being executed is abort
EOF         Handles the receipt of EOF as a command.
a(rgs)         Print the argument list of the current function.
retval         Print the return value for the last return of a function.
p expression         Print the value of the expression.
pp expression         Pretty-print the value of the expression.
l(ist) [first [,last] | .]          List source code for the current file.  With
longlist | ll         List the whole source code for the current function or fra
source expression         Try to get source code for the given object and displa
whatis arg         Print the type of the argument.
display [expression]          Display the value of the expression if it changed,
undisplay [expression]          Do not display the expression any more in the cu
interact          Start an interactive interpreter whose global namespace
alias [name [command [parameter parameter ...] ]]         Create an alias called
unalias name         Delete the specified alias.
h(elp)         Without argument, print the list of available commands.         W
(!) statement         Execute the (one-line) statement in the context of the cur
